channel.title: e/acc — the acceleration log
channel.description: Frontier model releases, compute buildouts and culture moments — the AI acceleration, dated and sourced. Updated weekly by e-acc.ai.

item.title: [frontier] Claude Sonnet 5 ships
item.description: Anthropic's most agentic Sonnet becomes the default model — near-Opus performance at Sonnet speed and price, aimed squarely at running agents cheaply.

item.title: GPT-5.6 previews under federal gate
item.description: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 — Sol, Terra and Luna — but access is limited to ~20 US-government-vetted partners pending a pre-release review: the frontier's first regulatory chokepoint.

item.title: [frontier] Claude Fable 5 ships
item.description: Anthropic opens the Claude 5 family: always-on adaptive thinking and a 1M-token context window.

item.title: [frontier] GPT-5.5 ships
item.description: OpenAI's flagship update lands in ChatGPT and Codex; API access follows a day later.

item.title: [frontier] Claude Opus 4.5 ships
item.description: Anthropic's strongest coding model to date lands days after Gemini 3 — the frontier cadence compresses to weeks.

item.title: [frontier] Gemini 3 ships
item.description: Google's frontier line jumps again; agentic coding and long-horizon tasks improve another step.

item.title: [frontier] GPT-5.1 ships
item.description: OpenAI iterates the GPT-5 line with adaptive reasoning effort and faster default paths.

item.title: [frontier] GPT-5 ships
item.description: OpenAI unifies reasoning and fast paths behind one router; frontier access goes mass-market.

item.title: [frontier] Claude 4 ships
item.description: Anthropic's Opus 4 and Sonnet 4 push sustained autonomous coding into production use.

item.title: Stargate announced: $500B
item.description: SoftBank, OpenAI, Oracle and MGX commit $500B over four years — $100B deploying immediately — for US AI datacenters.

item.title: [frontier] DeepSeek-R1 open weights
item.description: Frontier-class reasoning released under MIT license at a fraction of incumbent training cost.

item.title: [frontier] OpenAI o1: the reasoning era
item.description: Test-time compute becomes the new scaling axis — models that think before they answer.

item.title: [frontier] GPT-4o: omni goes realtime
item.description: Native audio-vision-text at half the price of GPT-4 Turbo. Frontier intelligence keeps getting cheaper.

item.title: [frontier] Claude 3 ships
item.description: Opus matches GPT-4; for the first time the frontier is contested by more than one lab.

item.title: The Techno-Optimist Manifesto
item.description: Marc Andreessen publishes the manifesto; e/acc ideas enter mainstream venture discourse.

item.title: [frontier] GPT-4 ships
item.description: The capability jump that reset every timeline. $30 per million input tokens at launch.

item.title: ChatGPT launches
item.description: 100M users in two months. The acceleration becomes visible to everyone.

item.title: e/acc is born
item.description: A pseudonymous newsletter by @zestular, @BasedBeffJezos, @bayeslord and co. names the movement: build, accelerate, ascend the Kardashev gradient.
